horses.name: ORGANISE
horses.reason: This horse arrives unbeaten in two career starts, winning both comfortably. Multiple tipsters including Racing Post, Timeform and Paddy Power have all selected him. The Gosden yard won this race 12 months ago and the trainer is in sparkling form. James Doyle keeps the ride after their recent Yarmouth success and the odds have shortened from 11/4 to 11/10, suggesting strong market confidence.

horses.name: ZEUS OLYMPIOS
horses.reason: An unbeaten four-year-old arriving here with a perfect 4-4 record. He comfortably beat re-opposing Opera Ballo by two and a quarter lengths in the Joel Stakes last time, and meets that rival on better terms today. Karl Burke's yard is striking at 28% and Ryan Moore takes the ride. Several tipsters prefer him to the favourite Field Of Gold at these odds.

horses.name: ACTION
horses.reason: A Frankel colt who ran second to Hawk Mountain in the Futurity Trophy at Doncaster last autumn and showed progressive improvement through his juvenile season. Aidan O'Brien won this Classic Trial last year and tipsters expect this step up in trip to bring out the best in him. Ryan Moore in the saddle is always a positive.

horses.name: FEVER DREAM
horses.reason: The market leader in the Highland National with strong support. This staying chaser has shown consistent form in big-field handicaps and the good ground will suit. The odds have shortened from 4/1 to 100/30 which indicates money coming for him from well-informed sources.

horses.name: THE DOG THIEF
horses.reason: Racing Post have made this horse their NAP for this race, describing him as a promising 6-year-old who could be well ahead of his mark. A lightly-raced sort with scope for improvement, the step up in trip looks ideal and he's attracted plenty of tipster support at each-way odds.

horses.name: MR JUKEBOX
horses.reason: Won his handicap debut by five lengths last week in dominant fashion, making a mockery of his opening mark. Although he carries a 3lb penalty, he's described as 'well-in' under that weight and the Racing Post tip says he'll take some stopping if anywhere near the same form. Warren Greatrex in good form.
